title: Fillet on selected edge(s) of a body
source: OneCAD-CPP/src/app/history/RegenerationEngine.cpp:1285-1349 — buildFillet: real BRepFilletAPI_MakeFillet(targetShape) over resolved edge ids; guards 'Fillet requires body input' (:1302), 'Fillet radius too small' (:1315), 'Fillet operation failed (radius too large?)' (:1343-1345).
OneCAD-CPP/src/app/document/OperationRecord.h:114-120 — FilletChamferParams {mode, radius, edgeIds (ElementMap edge ids), chainTangentEdges}.
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:220-268 — Test 2 dependency ordering: extrude(op1)->fillet(op2 uses body1); topo sort op1 before op2; downstream(op1)=[op2]; upstream(op2)=[op1].
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:270-312 — Test 3 suppression/failure tracking with a fillet node.
OneCAD-CPP/src/core/modeling/EdgeChainer.h + CLAUDE.md — chainTangentEdges auto-expands the selection to a tangent-continuous edge chain (normal dot, tangent continuity).

OneCAD-CPP/src/kernel/elementmap/ElementMap.h:212-264 — resolveWithFallback: how a (possibly stale) edge id is resolved to a live edge (literal hit score 0; else descriptor re-match within maxScore; refuse on symmetric tie).

opScript.note: Produces body_1 (a box). Provides selectable edges.

intent.descriptor: frozen ElementDescriptor of the picked edge (14-field, see ElementMap.h:58-70)

opScript.note: Fillet a single selected vertical edge, radius 1.0. edgeIds carry snapshot-scoped TopoKeys / ElementIds; the worker resolves each through the ladder (SCHEMA §10).

topology.note: NO current OneCAD-CPP prototype asserts fillet volume or face/edge counts. buildFillet is real (BRepFilletAPI) and used by the app, but the only fillet coverage in tests is dependency-graph ordering with placeholder edge ids ('edge1','edge2') that are NOT executed by OCCT. A single-edge fillet on a box adds 1 rolled face and rounds the edge; capture exact counts/volume from the new worker rather than inventing them here.

radiusTooSmall.outcome: op fails, error 'Fillet radius too small'

radiusTooLarge.outcome: op fails, error 'Fillet operation failed (radius too large?)'
radiusTooLarge.newStackMapping: worker terminal resp error.code OP_FAILED / GEOMETRY_INVALID (recoverable, session intact) SCHEMA §8

noBodyInput.outcome: op fails, error 'Fillet requires body input'

notes: This case establishes the fillet op SHAPE and reference-resolution path, not a numeric geometry oracle (the old stack has none for executed fillets). It is the setup body for case_e (naming break) and case_f (symmetric ambiguity). chainTangentEdges=true is the old default (OperationRecord.h:119). In the new stack each edge in edgeIds also carries a semanticRef (intent descriptor + anchor) so the ladder can rebind after upstream edits.
